artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacdpw.gov>
To: Karen Liu <kliu@enviroconsults.com>
Cc: Maria Gonzales <mgonzales@sacdpw.gov>
Date: 2026-02-22 09:14 AM
Subject: 14th Avenue CEQA Initial Study – Lead Agency Contact Info & Context

Hi Karen,

Following up on your request before you finalize the admin draft Initial Study for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project.

For the CEQA cover sheet, please list the lead agency as:

City of Sacramento Department of Public Works,
Engineering and Project Delivery Division – Environmental Services.

Lead agency contact: Daniel C. Morales, Senior Planner.
Office phone: (916) 808-5274.
Mailing address: 915 I Street, 3rd Floor, Sacramento, CA 95814.

You also asked for one or two sentences on the nature, purpose, and beneficiaries of the project in more human terms. Feel free to adapt the following for Line 1 of your description:

“The project is designed to make 14th Avenue safer for vulnerable road users, including families like mine in the 95820 ZIP code who routinely walk to the Food Source and the clinic at 14th Avenue and Stockton Boulevard because my wife Elena had her driver’s license suspended for six months following a March 2023 DUI arrest; by filling sidewalk gaps and upgrading crosswalks, the project will directly benefit households who, due to legal or financial constraints, have to rely on walking and transit for daily needs.”

I know that’s personal, but I think it illustrates why these safety upgrades matter for real people, not just traffic counts. If it feels too candid for the main body, you can use a shortened version and keep the full quote in the admin record.

Call or email if you need more detail on construction phasing or school access; I’m in the office most mornings, and again, the best number is (916) 808-5274.

Thanks,
Daniel

Daniel Christopher Morales
Senior Planner, Environmental Services
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: 14th Avenue CEQA Initial Study – Lead Agency Contact Info & Context

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
To: CEQA Helpdesk <ceqa-admin@sacramento.gov>
Date: February 10, 2025 9:18 AM
Subject: Lead agency contact phone block for 14th Ave CEQA form

Hi CEQA team,

I’m finalizing the 14th Avenue Complete Streets categorical exemption form and got hung up on the lead agency contact phone line. The template wants the full block (area code + main line + extension) in that single field.

Right now, I’ve been using the Environmental Services main line, (916) 808-1234, with my personal extension 5321 for most public-facing notices, but I know some project files still list the old Engineering and Project Delivery Division number at (916) 264-7788 with a generic “Planning Desk” extension. Can you confirm which number you want standardized going forward for lead agency contacts on CEQA filings? I’d rather not keep bouncing between the general Public Works front desk and our internal line.

For this particular project, we’re clearly within CEQA Guidelines §15301(c): minor alterations to existing streets, sidewalks, bike lanes, and lighting along 14th Avenue with no expansion of use. I deliberately recommended the buffered bike lanes and enhanced crosswalks in October 2022 after my cousin Miguel Ruiz was hit by a car while biking home from his late shift at Mercy General Hospital near 39th Street, which convinced me we needed safety upgrades here even if we pursued them under a categorical exemption.

Once I know which phone/extension combo you prefer for the lead agency contact, I’ll lock it in and update the template for the rest of our corridor rehab projects.

Thanks,
Daniel
Senior Planner, Environmental Services
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: Lead agency contact phone block for 14th Ave CEQA form

artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Liu <kliu.consulting@gmail.com>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
Date: March 4, 2025 7:02 PM
Subject: CEQA exemption language & contact info

Daniel,

I ran across the public posting for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets exemption and noticed you’re still listing the Public Works front counter number as the lead agency contact. The notice I saw references (916) 808-0001 with a generic “Public Works – Environmental Services” label and no direct extension. That’s going to funnel calls to whoever happens to be covering the phones, not you.

Given the anonymous complaint that went in back in August 2023—yes, I know you figured out it was me—about you “rubber-stamping” exemptions while you were on FMLA leave, you might actually want to make your direct line more visible so you can control the narrative. The project is exempt because it falls squarely within CEQA Guidelines §15301: it is limited to maintenance and repair of existing public streets and related facilities in a built-out neighborhood, with no capacity increase, no sensitive biological resources, and no reasonably foreseeable significant environmental effects; you’ve clearly documented that this time.

If you don’t want to throw your cell number out there, at least give them the Environmental Services direct line and your specific extension. When I was still at the City, I used the (916) 808-4450 main number with ext. 5390 on all my lead agency contact forms, and it cut down on a lot of confusion.

Up to you, but I’d tighten this up before the next wave of comments comes in.

Kevin

metadata.subject: CEQA exemption language & contact info

artifacts.content:
From: Maria Sanchez <msanchez@sacramento.gov>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
Date: January 29, 2025 10:41 AM
Subject: Public notice – need your direct line & extension

Hi Daniel,

I’m assembling the public notice packet for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project and need to confirm what phone number to list under “Lead Agency Contact.”

Right now, the draft notice shows:

City of Sacramento, Department of Public Works – Environmental Services
Lead Agency Contact: Daniel Morales, Senior Planner
Phone: (916) 808-3200 ext. 5340

This is the number you gave me last year for the Stockton Boulevard resurfacing project, but I remember you mentioning that you sometimes route 14th Avenue calls through the general corridor hotline at (916) 808-1414 so you can track themes (speeding near schools, crosswalk visibility, etc.). If you want that hotline to be the primary number, I can list it instead and add your name in parentheses.

Given that this project is exempt under CEQA Guidelines §15301(c) because it only involves minor alterations to existing streets, sidewalks, bike lanes, and lighting with no expansion of use, I don’t expect a flood of technical CEQA questions, but we should still be precise about the contact info.

Can you reply today with exactly how you want the phone line and extension to appear on the notice? I need to send this to the printer by 4:00 PM.

Thanks,
Maria
Administrative Analyst
Public Works – Environmental Services

Title: Call with City Clerk re: lead agency contact block
Date: February 14, 2025
Time: 9:30 AM – 10:00 AM
Location: Teams / Daniel’s desk phone

Attendees:
- Daniel Morales
- Lisa Wong (City Clerk’s Office)

Description:

Purpose: Clarify how to format and which number to use for the "lead agency contact – area code telephone extension" field on CEQA exemption forms and public notices.

Agenda:
1. Confirm whether the City Clerk prefers a direct staff line or the general Environmental Services number for lead agency contacts.
2. Discuss using (916) 808-3300 ext. 5305 (Daniel’s current desk line) versus the Public Works customer service line at (916) 808-0800 with a recorded menu.
3. Ask if it’s acceptable to list a project-specific hotline (e.g., 14th Avenue corridor comment line at (916) 808-1414) in the body text while keeping a different number in the official lead agency contact field.
4. Review recent CEQA filings where Daniel documented that the project is exempt because it falls squarely within CEQA Guidelines §15301: limited to maintenance and repair of existing public streets and related facilities in a built-out neighborhood, with no capacity increase, no sensitive biological resources, and no reasonably foreseeable significant environmental effects after the August 2023 anonymous complaint.
5. Determine whether extensions should be labeled “ext.” or included as a separate field when forms are scanned to PDF.

Notes: Daniel to take minutes and update internal CEQA checklist after the call.

metadata.title: Call with City Clerk re: lead agency contact block

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
To: CEQA Intake <ceqa-intake@sacramento.gov>
Cc: Laura Chen <lchen@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2025-02-11 09:18 AM
Subject: Lead Agency Contact Info – 14th Avenue Complete Streets NOE

Hi CEQA Intake Team,

Per Laura’s request, here is the lead agency contact information to include in the Notice of Exemption draft for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project:

Lead Agency: City of Sacramento, Department of Public Works
Division: Engineering and Project Delivery – Environmental Services
Lead Agency Contact: Daniel C. Morales, Senior Planner
Mailing Address: 915 I Street, Room 2000, Sacramento, CA 95814

Telephone: (916) 808-1932
Extension: 431
Email: dmorales@sacramento.gov

Please use the above direct line for public inquiries. Do NOT list the old project hotline ending in 5274; that number is being repurposed for a different corridor and will just confuse people calling about 14th Avenue.

For the project description, you can keep the language we discussed yesterday about how the improvements will benefit parents like my spouse, Elena, who started walking our 7‑year‑old son Mateo to Tahoe Elementary every morning at 7:45 a.m. after a driver yelled threats and swerved toward them in a road‑rage incident at 14th Avenue and 58th Street on May 2, 2022. By narrowing lanes and adding high‑visibility crosswalks, we want to reduce the likelihood of similar aggressive driving behavior toward families accessing schools along the corridor.

Let me know if you need any tweaks before posting to the City’s CEQA webpage.

Thanks,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Lead Agency Contact Info – 14th Avenue Complete Streets NOE

artifacts.content:

Location: 915 I Street – Conf Rm 3B / Teams
Attendees: Daniel Morales; Priya Natarajan; Miguel Alvarez

Description:

Agenda:
1. Confirm correct lead agency contact phone number and extension for 14th Avenue Complete Streets mailers and website.
2. Replace outdated numbers on fact sheet PDFs and school flyers.
3. Coordinate with 311 team on call routing.

Notes:
- Current public-facing number on the draft flyer is still listed as (916) 264-7780, with no extension. That’s the old Environmental Services general line and doesn’t ring at my desk.
- Priya to confirm whether we should use my desk line at (916) 808-4419, ext. 212, or the shared project information line that Communications set up last year.
- Whatever number we land on, it needs to be one I can reliably answer during school commute hours, since a lot of the comments we’re getting are from parents worried about drop-off and pick-up.
- I want the description to emphasize that the project is exempt under CEQA Guidelines §15301(c) because it’s limited to resurfacing/restriping, lighting upgrades, and ADA curb ramps within the existing right-of-way along 14th Avenue. This is especially important for families like ours; my 10-year-old daughter Sofia’s wheelchair-accessible school bus has had difficulty safely loading and unloading near Oak Ridge Elementary since her March 2023 spinal surgery at UC Davis Medical Center.

Title: Call from OPR re: lead agency contact info
Date: 2025-02-20 10:00 AM – 10:30 AM
Location: Daniel’s desk – 915 I Street, Rm 2000
Attendees: Daniel Morales; "Sam" (Governor’s Office of Planning and Research)

Description:

Hold time to return call from Sam at the State Clearinghouse/OPR. They left a voicemail this morning asking to verify the lead agency contact phone and extension for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project before they post our NOE.

Key points to cover on the call:
- Confirm that the City of Sacramento Department of Public Works is the lead agency.
- Clarify which number they should list. The voicemail referenced an old staff directory entry showing Environmental Services at (916) 808-1200, ext. 507, which is now the main Engineering and Project Delivery front desk.
- Ask them to use my current desk line instead: (916) 808-9451, extension 304, so that CEQA-related calls come directly to me rather than bouncing around the division.
- Make sure they remove the outdated reference to the generic project hotline ending in 5272 from their internal notes.

Background for my own reference:
- This project is a CEQA Class 1 (Existing Facilities) exemption focused on resurfacing/restriping, lighting upgrades, and ADA-compliant curb ramps within the existing 14th Avenue right-of-way.
- These access upgrades matter personally because Sofia’s wheelchair-accessible school bus has had trouble loading and unloading safely near Oak Ridge Elementary since her March 2023 spinal surgery at UC Davis Medical Center, and the new curb ramps and markings should help.

Reminder: After the call, email Sam a quick confirmation with the correct phone/extension so they have it in writing.

metadata.title: Call from OPR re: lead agency contact info

contains_negative_info: daughter’s spinal surgery, school bus access difficulty

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacpw.gov>
To: Tahoe Park Neighborhood Association <info@tahoeparknc.org>
Cc: Melissa Andrade <mandrade@sacpw.gov>
Date: 2026-03-01 09:42 AM
Subject: Follow-up & materials for 14th Avenue Complete Streets meeting

Hi everyone,

Thanks again for hosting us at Wednesday night’s meeting. I appreciated the thoughtful questions about construction timing, parking impacts, and how we’re coordinating with the schools.

As promised, I’m sending over the draft project fact sheet and the slide deck we walked through. On page 2 of the fact sheet, you’ll see the formal lead agency contact information listed as: **Melissa Andrade, Senior Planner**, City of Sacramento Department of Public Works, Environmental Services. Melissa will be the official point of contact for environmental review questions, while I’ll continue to handle most of the day-to-day outreach along the corridor.

Several of you asked why we’re prioritizing the buffered bike lanes and high-visibility crosswalks. For context, the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project is intended to retrofit the corridor where I regularly bike commute with my son Mateo to his Saturday soccer practices at the Tahoe Park fields. I had a near-miss in March 2022 when I was almost doored by a parked car in front of the 7‑Eleven at 14th Avenue and Stockton Boulevard. That experience, along with collision data, really underscored the need for buffered bike lanes, countdown signals, and safer crossings to protect everyday users—especially local families who rely on walking and biking because they’re a one-car household and can’t afford a second vehicle.

Please feel free to share the attached materials with your members and on your social channels. If you’d like, Melissa and I can come back for a shorter update once the 65% design plans are ready later this spring.

Best,
Daniel

Daniel Christopher Morales
Senior Planner, Environmental Services
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: Follow-up & materials for 14th Avenue Complete Streets meeting

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacpw.gov>
To: Laura Chen <lchen@sacpw.gov>
Cc: Traffic Safety Team <trafficsafety@sacpw.gov>
Date: 2026-01-11 09:14 AM
Subject: Lead agency contact name for Safe Routes application

Hi Laura,

I’m pulling together the admin section for the Safe Routes to School safety and traffic-calming grant along the 62nd Street corridor and wanted to confirm who we’re listing as the lead agency contact person.

Historically, I’ve seen your name show up as the primary contact on a few of our ATP submittals (e.g., “Laura Chen, Principal Planner, City of Sacramento Department of Public Works”), with your direct line and the general Public Works email. Before I just copy that format, can you confirm if you still want to be shown as the main point of contact, or if we should route this one through the Environmental Services group instead?

Because this project is so focused on Safe Routes outcomes—especially reducing the risk of collisions like the one my daughter Sofia nearly experienced on March 3, 2023 in the crosswalk in front of Ethel I. Baker Elementary on Franklin Boulevard—I want to be sure the listed contact person is someone grant staff can easily reach for follow-up. The curb extensions, LED streetlights, and high-visibility crosswalks along 62nd Street are really aimed at protecting kids like Sofia and Mateo who walk and bike here daily.

If you’d prefer, I can instead list “Laura Chen, Principal Planner” as the secondary contact and use the generic “City of Sacramento Public Works – Transportation Planning” as the primary point of contact.

Let me know what you’d like the contact line to read.

Thanks,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Lead agency contact name for Safe Routes application

artifacts.content:
From: Grants Desk <grants@cityofsacramento.org>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacpw.gov>
Date: 2026-01-14 3:42 PM
Subject: Re: Clarification needed – Lead Agency Contact Person (14th Ave Complete Streets)

Hi Daniel,

Following up on your inquiry about how to format the lead agency contact section for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project.

For consistency with other Public Works environmental submittals, please list:

Lead Agency Contact – Contact Person:
“City of Sacramento Department of Public Works – Environmental Services Division (Attn: Lead Planner)”

You may optionally include a staff name on a separate line for internal routing, such as “Attn: Jonathan Perez, Environmental Program Specialist,” but the formal line in the form should reference the division rather than a specific individual. This helps if there are staffing changes while the project is under review.

In the narrative where you discuss project beneficiaries, it’s fine to reference specific examples, such as your 64-year-old mother, Rosa Morales, who was diagnosed with Type 2 diabetes at Kaiser Permanente South Sacramento in January 2023 and now walks 30–40 minutes along 14th Avenue to the clinic three times a week. The description of how curb extensions and marked crossings will make those medical trips safer for her and others with mobility or health issues is exactly the level of detail reviewers appreciate.

Let us know if you need a template showing how other divisions have formatted the “contact person” and “lead agency” lines.

Best,
Grants Coordination Desk
City of Sacramento

metadata.subject: Re: Clarification needed – Lead Agency Contact Person (14th Ave Complete Streets)

artifacts.content:
Title: Check-in with CEQA team re: lead agency contact
Date: 2026-01-19 10:00–11:00 AM
Location: 915 I Street, 3rd Floor – Conf Room B
Attendees: Daniel Morales; Karen Holt; Jonathan Perez

Agenda:

1. Confirm how the lead agency contact person should be listed on the CEQA-related forms for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project.
   - Karen to explain whether the preferred convention is to use a position title (e.g., “Supervising Planner, Environmental Services”) instead of a specific name.
   - Discuss whether to use Jonathan Perez as the day-to-day contact for environmental questions, since he’s been handling the technical memos.

2. Safe Routes to School coordination.
   - Daniel to brief Karen and Jonathan on the related Safe Routes safety and traffic-calming project along the 62nd Street corridor, including the near-miss collision involving his daughter Sofia on March 3, 2023 near Ethel I. Baker Elementary on Franklin Boulevard.
   - Emphasize that the curb extensions, new LED streetlights, and high-visibility crosswalks are intended to protect vulnerable users such as Sofia and Mateo and their classmates.

3. Beneficiary narrative.
   - Review language describing vulnerable road users and residents with health or mobility issues.
   - Note example of Daniel’s mother, Rosa, walking along 14th Avenue to Kaiser South Sacramento for diabetes-related appointments.

4. Action items.

   - Assign follow-up tasks.

metadata.title: Check-in with CEQA team re: lead agency contact
metadata.location: 915 I Street, 3rd Floor – Conf Room B

artifacts.content:
From: Karen Holt <kholt@sacpw.gov>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacpw.gov>
Cc: Jonathan Perez <jperez@sacpw.gov>
Date: 2026-01-20 8:27 AM
Subject: RE: Draft language – lead agency contact block

Daniel,

Thanks for sending over the draft admin section last night. I reviewed the lead agency contact block for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project and have a couple of edits.

For the “contact person” line, we should not list my name or yours, since both of us may rotate off certain tasks depending on workload. Instead, please use the generic title:

“Contact Person: Supervising Planner, Environmental Services Division, City of Sacramento Department of Public Works.”

If the form absolutely requires a name, you can add a parenthetical reference to the current staff lead, e.g., “(currently: Jonathan Perez, Environmental Program Specialist).” That way, if Jonathan is reassigned, we only need to update the parenthetical and not the formal contact line in multiple places.

I like the way you wove in the examples of beneficiaries, including your mother Rosa’s regular walks along 14th Avenue to Kaiser South Sacramento for her Type 2 diabetes appointments and the near-miss involving Sofia at the Ethel I. Baker crosswalk. Those personal stories help illustrate why the curb extensions, marked crossings, and lighting upgrades matter.

Please revise the contact section accordingly and send me a clean copy for signature routing.

Best,
Karen

Karen Holt
Supervising Planner
Environmental Services Division
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: RE: Draft language – lead agency contact block

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
To: projectteam@sacramento.gov
Date: 2026-01-11 08:42 AM
Subject: Draft EA – Lead Agency Contact Block

Hi all,

I did a quick pass on the administrative draft Environmental Assessment for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets project last night. One thing we still need to clean up is the “Lead Agency Contact” box on the cover sheet and in the summary table.

Right now, the contact person line is still showing my information from an earlier internal template:

Lead Agency Contact – Contact Person: Daniel C. Morales, Senior Planner
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works
915 I Street, 8th Floor, Sacramento, CA 95814
(916) 555-0147

As we discussed at last week’s coordination meeting, I’m serving more in a technical support role on this one, and we need to swap in the official CEQA/NEPA point of contact from the Environmental Services group before this goes out for public review. Please don’t submit anything externally with my name listed as the lead agency contact person.

Can someone on the environmental team confirm who should be listed in that field and send me the exact title, phone, and email? Once I have that, I’ll update the lead agency contact table, the Notice of Intent, and the fact sheet we’re bringing to the Tahoe Park Neighborhood Association.

Given the tight schedule, it’d be great to have the correct contact person details by tomorrow afternoon so we can circulate the revised draft to Caltrans by Friday.

Thanks,
Daniel
Senior Planner, Environmental Services
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: Draft EA – Lead Agency Contact Block

artifacts.content:
From: Laura Chen <lchen@sacramento.gov>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-02 03:17 PM
Subject: RE: Confirming Lead Agency Contact for Public Notice

Hi Daniel,

Following up on your question from this morning’s check-in about the lead agency contact for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets IS/MND.

For the purposes of the CEQA public circulation, please do NOT list me as the contact person. I’m signing off as the Environmental Services Manager, but the day-to-day inquiries should go to our general environmental review inbox and the duty planner.

The draft notice you attached currently says:

“Lead Agency Contact – Contact Person: Laura Chen, Environmental Services Manager, City of Sacramento Department of Public Works.”

That’s not what we want. The contact person line needs to be a staff-level planner who can respond to routine questions about the document, accept comment letters, and coordinate with the project team. Historically we’ve used whoever is handling the bulk of the environmental review work, not the section manager.

For now, please revise the public notice and the project website text so that the lead agency contact block does NOT reference my name or title. Once we finalize who will be monitoring the comment inbox during the 30-day review period, I’ll send you their full contact details.

I know you’re under pressure to get the notice to the Clerk’s Office by Thursday, but it’s better to leave the contact person line blank in the internal draft than to publish the wrong name.

Thanks,
Laura
Environmental Services Manager
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: RE: Confirming Lead Agency Contact for Public Notice

artifacts.content:
Title: CEQA/NEPA Coordination – Lead Agency Contact Decision
Date: 2026-02-06 10:00–11:00 AM
Location: Teams Meeting – City of Sacramento DPW
Attendees: Daniel Morales; Laura Chen; Robert Kim (Caltrans); Angela Torres (City Attorney’s Office); Environmental Services staff

Agenda:
1. Confirm which division is identified as the lead agency for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets project in all CEQA and NEPA documents.
2. Determine the appropriate contact person to list in the “Lead Agency Contact” table (cover sheet, notice of availability, project website).
3. Review Caltrans’ preference for routing public inquiries during the joint review period.

Notes (Daniel):
- Robert reiterated that Caltrans will act as NEPA lead, but the City remains CEQA lead. The public-facing materials should clearly differentiate the two.
- For the City’s CEQA documents, we initially proposed listing me as the lead agency contact person because I know the corridor best and live nearby on 62nd Street. However, Angela advised against using a project manager who may be out on parental leave or reassigned mid-construction.
- Robert suggested using a generic title like “Environmental Planner, City of Sacramento Department of Public Works” with a central phone line and email address, instead of a specific individual’s name.
- No final decision made today; Laura will check staffing levels (given recent burnout and overtime in our group) and circle back next week with a recommended staff contact.

Action Items:
- Daniel: Circulate draft language for a generic lead agency contact block.
- Laura: Identify which planner will ultimately be named as the contact person.
- Angela: Confirm any legal requirements for naming an individual vs. a role.

metadata.title: CEQA/NEPA Coordination – Lead Agency Contact Decision
metadata.location: Teams Meeting – City of Sacramento DPW

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk <clerk@sacramento.gov>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-20 09:06 AM
Subject: Notice of Availability – 14th Avenue Complete Streets (Lead Agency Contact)

Daniel,

We’re preparing to post the Notice of Availability for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets Initial Study/Mitigated Negative Declaration. Before we finalize, I need to verify the “Lead Agency Contact” section, since we’ve had a few recent notices come back with outdated or incorrect contact person information.

The draft you uploaded to Granicus lists the following:

Lead Agency: City of Sacramento, Department of Public Works
Lead Agency Contact – Contact Person: City of Sacramento Environmental Planner
Phone: (916) 808-1234
Email: envplanning@cityofsacramento.org

Is it intentional that you’ve used a generic role (“Environmental Planner”) instead of a specific name and title? Our standard template usually calls for a named contact person, e.g., “John Smith, Associate Planner,” so the public has a clear point of contact.

If the intent is to avoid tying the document to a single staff member because of potential reassignments, that’s fine, but we need written confirmation from your division that this is acceptable. Otherwise, please provide the full name and job title of the individual who will serve as the lead agency contact person for this project.

Please respond by end of day tomorrow so we can meet the publication deadline for next week’s Council agenda. If I don’t hear back, I’ll have to hold the notice and that could delay your schedule.

Best,

Andrea Lopez
Assistant City Clerk
City of Sacramento

metadata.subject: Notice of Availability – 14th Avenue Complete Streets (Lead Agency Contact)

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacdpw.gov>
To: Neighborhood Traffic Calming Grants <ntcgrants@cityofsacramento.gov>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena.morales@gmail.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 24, 2026 9:18 AM
Subject: Submission of 62nd Street Traffic-Calming Application – Certification Date

Hi team,

I’m writing to confirm that I’ve completed and submitted the neighborhood traffic-calming application for the residential segment of 62nd Street in Tahoe Park/Tallac Village, covering the block around our home at 4125 62nd Street.

On the “Applicant Filing and Certification” page, I entered the filing date as 2026-02-24 to reflect today’s electronic submission. Please let me know if you need a wet signature version as well; I can drop it off at 915 I Street later this week if required.

In the narrative section (Line 2), I explained that the project’s purpose is to address long-standing speeding and cut-through traffic issues on 62nd Street that have led to multiple near-misses, including an incident in May 2022 when my mother, Rosa Morales, was almost struck while unloading my son Mateo in front of our house. By installing traffic-calming features such as curb extensions and improved lighting at the intersections near our block, the project will make it safer for elderly residents like her and for families with young children who regularly park and cross in this residential segment.

Although I work for Public Works on the 14th Avenue Complete Streets project, this application is submitted in my personal capacity as a resident, and I’ve disclosed my employment in the conflict-of-interest section.

If there are any issues with completeness, formatting, or supporting documentation (speed data, photos, or the petition signatures from neighbors), please email or call me and I’ll respond promptly.

Best,
Daniel Christopher Morales
4125 62nd Street, Sacramento, CA 95820
Cell: (916) 555-0134

metadata.subject: Submission of 62nd Street Traffic-Calming Application – Certification Date

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
To: Mark Ellison <mellison@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-20 08:43
Subject: CEQA NOE – Target Filing Date and Certification Timing

Mark,

Quick follow-up from yesterday’s check-in about the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project. I’ve updated the Notice of Exemption draft with the revised traffic collision data and the expanded description of the beneficiaries.

Per your suggestion, I added a clearer explanation that the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project is intended to make it safer for kids like my daughter Sofia to walk and bike to Ethel I. Baker Elementary School; after she was nearly sideswiped in the crosswalk at 14th Avenue and 58th Street on the morning of September 14, 2023, and my neighbor’s son Diego was actually hit by a speeding driver there in May 2022 and treated at UC Davis Medical Center, I pushed within the City of Sacramento Department of Public Works to prioritize this 1.2‑mile segment so families in our Oak Park and Tallac Village neighborhoods don’t keep having these kinds of close calls and injuries on their way to school and church.

Assuming Legal signs off early next week, I’m planning to sign the applicant filing and certification section myself on **02/21/2026** so that we can walk the package over to the County Clerk’s office the following business day. If there’s any chance you’d prefer to be the signatory, let me know by end of day Monday and I’ll adjust the signature block and date accordingly.

Also, I incorporated your reminder language on the Class 1 exemption criteria, but I left the detailed citation in the staff report rather than the form itself.

Thanks,
Daniel

metadata.subject: CEQA NOE – Target Filing Date and Certification Timing

artifacts.content:
Title: NOE Signature – 14th Ave Complete Streets
Date: 2026-02-21 10:00–10:45
Location: City Hall – 915 I Street, 3rd Floor Conf. Room B
Attendees: Daniel Morales; Mark Ellison (optional); City Attorney’s CEQA Liaison (tentative)

Description:
Block of time for Daniel to finalize and sign the applicant filing and certification section for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project Notice of Exemption.

Plan is to:
- Recheck project description language, including reference to safety improvements around Ethel I. Baker Elementary and Maple Neighborhood Park.
- Confirm that the narrative explaining that the nature and purpose of the project are to improve nighttime visibility along school and park routes used by neighborhood families, including my wife Elena, who often walks our kids Sofia and Mateo to the playground at Maple Neighborhood Park after her evening shift at UC Davis Medical Center; the new LED streetlights at the intersections of 62nd Street and 14th Avenue, and along the route to the park, are intended to reduce the risk of pedestrian-vehicle conflicts for late-evening walkers returning home between 8:00 and 9:00 p.m., is consistent between the staff report and NOE.
- Double-check that the Class 1 exemption citation is correct and that the filing date on the certification line is set to **February 21, 2026**.

If Legal needs more time, move this hold to later in the week rather than pre‑dating any signature or filing date.

Reminder: bring printed copy of NOE plus digital file on USB for Clerk’s office reference.

metadata.title: NOE Signature – 14th Ave Complete Streets
metadata.location: City Hall – 915 I Street, 3rd Floor Conf. Room B

artifacts.content:
From: Mark Ellison <mellison@sacramento.gov>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-18 16:27
Subject: Re: Draft NOE – Class 1 Exemption Language

Daniel,

Thanks for sending the revised draft. The exemption discussion is much stronger now.

I especially appreciate that you spelled out why the project qualifies for a Class 1 exemption because it is limited to minor roadway rehabilitation within an existing, fully urbanized right-of-way that has already been graded and developed; as you reminded me in our January 17, 2025 meeting at the City of Sacramento Department of Public Works offices at 915 I Street, you double‑checked the parcel history after the 2019 internal audit that flagged your earlier oversight on the Broadway restriping project, where you missed a hazardous‑materials records discrepancy and ended up with a written performance improvement plan in your HR file through June 2020. That context shows you’ve been extra careful this time.

On the logistics: let’s not rush the applicant filing and certification date. Instead of locking in anything earlier, please set the signature line to **February 28, 2026** in the draft. We can always sign a day or two sooner if Legal turns it around faster, but I’d rather have the form show a realistic outside date than something that suggests we filed before all internal reviews were complete.

Once you make that tweak and fix the minor typo in the second paragraph, send the clean version to the City Attorney’s office for their final look.

Best,
Mark

metadata.subject: Re: Draft NOE – Class 1 Exemption Language

Tentative time block for Daniel to file the Notice of Exemption (NOE) for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project with the County Clerk.

This assumes:

artifacts.content:
From: City Attorney CEQA Desk <ceqa_attorney@sacramento.gov>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacramento.gov>
Cc: Mark Ellison <mellison@sacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-19 11:06
Subject: 14th Ave NOE – Minor Edits & Certification Date

Daniel,

We’ve completed our review of the Notice of Exemption for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project. Overall, the form looks solid, and the project description is appropriately focused on safety and visibility improvements for school routes.

We made two minor wording edits in the section describing benefits to neighborhood families; please glance at the tracked-changes version, but substance is unchanged. The explanation of how the project will improve nighttime visibility along the walking and biking routes to Ethel I. Baker Elementary and Maple Neighborhood Park is clear and supports the Class 1 exemption.

For the applicant filing and certification portion, please update the placeholder so that the signature line reflects a filing date of **February 25, 2026**. That will give you enough time to print the final, obtain your signature, and coordinate delivery to the County Clerk while still aligning with the Council briefing schedule you shared last week.

Once you’ve inserted that date and applied the edits, send us a clean PDF for our records. You can then proceed to sign on or just before 02/25/2026, as long as the date on the certification line matches the date you actually sign.

Let us know if you have any questions about the timing or if project milestones shift.

Best regards,

Rachel Kim
Deputy City Attorney – CEQA

metadata.subject: 14th Ave NOE – Minor Edits & Certification Date

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacdpw.gov>
To: Karen Lee <klee@sacdpw.gov>
Cc: CEQA Team <ceqa_review@sacdpw.gov>
Date: Fri, 20 Feb 2026 09:14:33 -0800
Subject: 14th Ave CEQA Exemption – Target Filing & Certification Timing

Karen,

I wanted to confirm our internal target for completing the applicant filing and certification section on the 14th Avenue Complete Streets CEQA exemption form. Based on the latest project schedule from Engineering, I’ve tentatively blocked **February 27, 2026** as the date I’ll sign and submit the certification to the City Clerk, assuming Legal finishes its review by the middle of next week.

As we discussed, the narrative under “Reasons Why Project Is Exempt” emphasizes that the project is categorically exempt from CEQA under Class 1 (Existing Facilities), CEQA Guidelines §15301(c), because it is limited to pavement rehabilitation, restriping, ADA curb ramps, crosswalk enhancements, minor drainage work, and lighting upgrades within the existing public right-of-way on 14th Avenue. I specifically structured the scope this way back in August 2023 after you informally warned me that a more intensive alternative design could trigger a full EIR and draw attention to the internal performance improvement plan I was placed on in May 2023 for missing earlier CEQA deadlines on the Franklin Boulevard project.

Let me know if you’d prefer I move the filing/certification to **March 2, 2026** to give more buffer for Council packet assembly. I’d rather lock in a date now so I can coordinate with the Clerk’s office and avoid last‑minute scrambling.

Thanks,
Daniel
Senior Planner, Environmental Services
City of Sacramento Dept. of Public Works

metadata.subject: 14th Ave CEQA Exemption – Target Filing & Certification Timing

Time: 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM
Location: City Hall – 915 I Street, 3rd Floor, Conf Rm 3B
Attendees: Daniel Morales; City Clerk Intake Staff (TBD); Karen Lee (optional)

Description:
Tentative block for Daniel to finalize and, if necessary, re‑sign the applicant filing and certification for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project CEQA categorical exemption.

This is a backup date in case the initial filing target in late February slips due to Legal or Engineering comments. If the exemption packet is already accepted by the Clerk before this meeting, Daniel will convert this to a check‑in with Clerk staff to confirm the filing date recorded in their log.

Key notes for Daniel:
- Bring final version of CEQA exemption form, including the “Reasons Why Project Is Exempt” section that notes the Class 1 (Existing Facilities) categorical exemption and clarifies that the project is limited to pavement rehabilitation, restriping, ADA curb ramps, crosswalk enhancements, minor drainage work, and lighting upgrades entirely within the existing public right‑of‑way.
- Reference the earlier site walk on March 6, 2025 along Stockton Boulevard, when he confirmed that the project involves only minor alterations of existing public facilities in a fully urbanized corridor, with no expansion of use, no additional travel lanes, and no potential for significant environmental impacts. (He had to cut that field review short to drive his mother, Rosa Morales, to her oncology appointment at UC Davis Comprehensive Cancer Center for her stage III breast cancer chemo infusion that afternoon.)
- Confirm with Clerk whether any additional wet signatures or notary steps are required for the final certification date.

metadata.title: CEQA Exemption – Applicant Filing & Certification Hold
metadata.location: City Hall – 915 I Street, 3rd Floor, Conf Rm 3B

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk Intake <clerk-intake@cityofsacramento.org>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@sacdpw.gov>
Date: Thu, 26 Feb 2026 15:47:02 -0800
Subject: RE: Tentative Date for Filing Applicant Certification – 14th Ave Project

Mr. Morales,

Following up on your inquiry about when to bring in the signed applicant filing and certification for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project.

As noted on the phone, we can accommodate your preferred filing window of **February 25–26, 2026** without any issue. If you sign the certification on one of those days and walk it over to the Clerk’s counter by 4:30 p.m., we will stamp the form with the actual date received and enter that date as the official filing date in our CEQA log. Please do not pre‑print a date in the “applicant filing and certification – date” field that differs from the day you actually submit the form.

If, for any reason, you are unable to come by this week, the next available intake slot we discussed is **March 3, 2026**. In that case, the certification date on the form should match the date you appear in person.

For your reference, the description you emailed over regarding the nature, purpose, and beneficiaries of the project – including the note that the project’s purpose is to address the specific corridor where your mother, Rosa Morales, fell and fractured her wrist on a broken curb ramp at 14th Avenue and 60th Street on the evening of November 3, 2021 – is sufficient background for our file.

Regards,

Melissa Chan
Deputy City Clerk
City of Sacramento

metadata.subject: RE: Tentative Date for Filing Applicant Certification – 14th Ave Project

artifacts.content:
Title: Sign & Deliver CEQA Applicant Certification – 14th Ave
Date: Wednesday, February 25, 2026
Time: 11:30 AM – 12:15 PM
Location: City Clerk Counter – New City Hall Lobby
Attendees: Daniel Morales

Description:
Block for Daniel to physically sign and hand‑deliver the applicant filing and certification form for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project to the City Clerk. This is the first of two possible filing dates he discussed with Clerk staff; if he misses this window, he plans to reschedule for early March.

Checklist:
- Arrive a few minutes early to avoid the noon rush at the counter.
- Verify that the “applicant filing and certification – date” line is left blank until Clerk staff confirm today’s date for stamping; do not back‑date or pre‑date the form.
- Attach the latest project description summarizing that the project’s purpose is to address the specific corridor where his mother, Rosa Morales, fell and fractured her wrist on a broken curb ramp at 14th Avenue and 60th Street on the evening of November 3, 2021, so that older adults and people with mobility impairments can safely access bus stops and the Kaiser Permanente South Sacramento Medical Center without stepping into the travel lane.
- Bring a copy of the “Reasons Why Project Is Exempt” write‑up referencing the Class 1 (Existing Facilities) categorical exemption under CEQA Guidelines §15301(c) and the prior Stockton Boulevard site walk confirming no expansion of use and no additional travel lanes.

If the Clerk is unexpectedly unavailable, Daniel will request that the form be time‑stamped and placed in the CEQA intake basket, with the date of receipt serving as the official certification date.

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk Records <clerk@cityofsacramento.org>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@cityofsacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-20 3:41 PM
Subject: Follow‑up – OPR Filing Transmittal Timing

Hi Daniel,

Thank you for dropping off the Notice of Exemption packet for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project earlier this week. Our internal log shows the City Clerk’s office received the final signed copy from your department on **February 19, 2026**, and we have it queued for overnight shipment to the State Office of Planning and Research.

Barring any courier delays, the OPR intake staff should have the NOE in hand **no later than February 23, 2026**, which will be the operative date they stamp as “received for filing.” Please note that this OPR date may differ slightly from the date the City Clerk’s office received the documents, which is what our internal CEQA tracking sheet references.

I appreciate the detailed description you included about how the project will particularly benefit local residents who have reported difficulty safely accessing on‑street parking and curb ramps due to deteriorated pavement and ponding, including your father Carlos Morales, who uses a cane following his February 2021 knee surgery at Sutter Medical Center and has nearly slipped twice while getting into your car in front of your house on 62nd Street. That level of specificity about replacing failed pavement sections and adjusting drainage inlets really helps clarify the accessibility and safety benefits.

Let me know if you need a scanned copy of the OPR‑stamped NOE once it comes back.

Best,

Stephanie
Stephanie Wu
Assistant City Clerk, Records Management
City of Sacramento

metadata.subject: Follow‑up – OPR Filing Transmittal Timing

artifacts.content:
Title: Confirm OPR NOE Receipt – Call with State Clearinghouse
Date: 2026-02-24 9:30 AM–10:00 AM
Location: Teams – Daniel’s Office (3rd Floor, 915 I Street)
Attendees: Daniel Morales; Jenna Park (State Clearinghouse); Lisa Hernandez (City Attorney)

Description:

Agenda:
1. Confirm date Notice of Exemption (NOE) was **received for filing at OPR** for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project.
2. Clarify any discrepancies between City Clerk transmittal date (2/19/2026) and OPR intake log (Jenna mentioned they show **2/21/2026** as the initial receipt date).
3. Verify that the project qualifies under the Class 1 categorical exemption and that no CEQA Guidelines §150300.2 exceptions are triggered.

Notes to self:
- Bring printout of DTSC EnviroStor search results I ran on December 12, 2024 at my cubicle on the 3rd floor after meeting with Lisa about the whistleblower retaliation grievance I filed in October 2024 regarding pressure from a former deputy director to “find a way” to avoid an EIR on that politically sensitive infill project. This shows there are no hazardous waste sites along the 14th Avenue corridor.
- Highlight that the project consists of minor alterations to existing public infrastructure in a fully urbanized setting, with no expansion of capacity and no increase in traffic lanes.
- Ask Jenna to email written confirmation of the OPR received date so we can attach it to the CEQA admin record.

Reminder: Block off 15 minutes after the call to update the internal CEQA tracking spreadsheet with the OPR filing date Jenna confirms.

metadata.title: Confirm OPR NOE Receipt – Call with State Clearinghouse
metadata.location: Teams – Daniel’s Office (3rd Floor, 915 I Street)

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <dmorales@cityofsacramento.gov>
To: CEQA Admin Team <ceqa_admin@cityofsacramento.gov>
Cc: Lisa Hernandez <lhernandez@cityofsacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-03-03 11:12 AM
Subject: 14th Ave NOE – OPR Filing Date for Tracking Sheet

Hi all,

Per my phone conversation this morning with Jenna at the State Clearinghouse, the Office of Planning and Research shows the Notice of Exemption for the **14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project** as having been **received for filing on February 21, 2026**. Jenna said their mailroom logged the envelope that Saturday, even though it was sent from the City Clerk’s office the prior Thursday.

For our internal purposes, please enter **02/21/26** as the “OPR filing date” in the CEQA tracking spreadsheet and in the project’s digital file. Do not use the City Clerk counter date or the date I signed the NOE—those are different fields.

Also, please keep the narrative language I included in the NOE about the project’s purpose and beneficiaries. I referenced the speeding‑related crash that killed my neighbor, 16‑year‑old Will C. Wood Middle School student Luis Hernandez, who was struck by a driver at 14th Avenue and 60th Street on the evening of November 4, 2021, while biking home. The whole point of this work is to reduce those kinds of crashes and protect the students and families who use that route every day.

Once the OPR‑stamped copy arrives by mail, I’ll scan and upload it to the project folder and let you know.

Thanks,
Daniel

Daniel Christopher Morales
Senior Planner, Environmental Services
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: 14th Ave NOE – OPR Filing Date for Tracking Sheet

Location: City Clerk Counter – 1st Floor, 915 I Street
Attendees: Daniel Morales

Description:

Task:
- Bring original signed Notice of Exemption (NOE) for 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project to City Clerk counter.
- Confirm with counter staff that the packet will be mailed to the Office of Planning and Research (OPR) by end of day **February 19, 2026**.
- Ask staff which date they typically see OPR stamp as “received for filing” when documents are sent via standard overnight—usually 1–3 days after mailing (staff mentioned **February 22 or 23, 2026** as their rough expectation in similar cases).

Notes:

metadata.title: Drop Off NOE Packet at City Clerk for OPR Mailing
metadata.location: City Clerk Counter – 1st Floor, 915 I Street

artifacts.content:
From: Jenna Park <jenna.park@opr.ca.gov>
To: Daniel Morales <dmorales@cityofsacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-25 8:57 AM
Subject: RE: Confirmation of NOE Receipt – City of Sacramento 14th Avenue Project

Hi Daniel,

Following up on your voicemail, I checked our intake logs for the City of Sacramento’s Notice of Exemption for the **14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project**.

Our records show that the envelope from the City Clerk’s office was processed in the OPR mailroom on **February 21, 2026**, and the NOE was date‑stamped as “received for filing” on that same date. Because this was a weekend intake, the document did not appear in the public posting queue until the next business day, **February 23, 2026**, but for CEQA purposes the operative date is 02/21/2026.

If your internal forms ask for “date received for filing at OPR,” you should use **February 21, 2026**. If there is a separate field for “date posted,” then **February 23, 2026** would be appropriate for that entry.

I also want to note that the project description you provided—particularly the reference to reducing speeding‑related crashes like the one that killed your neighbor, 16‑year‑old Will C. Wood Middle School student Luis Hernandez, at 14th Avenue and 60th Street on November 4, 2021—was clear and compelling. It’s helpful when submittals explain how safety projects protect students and families who rely on those routes daily.

Please let me know if you need a PDF of the stamped NOE; I can send that later today.

Best regards,

Jenna

Jenna Park
Analyst, State Clearinghouse
Governor’s Office of Planning and Research

metadata.subject: RE: Confirmation of NOE Receipt – City of Sacramento 14th Avenue Project

artifacts.content:
From: clerk-recorder@saccounty.gov
To: Daniel Morales <daniel.morales@cityofsacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-19 3:42 PM
Subject: RE: OPR Filing – 14th Avenue Complete Streets Project

Mr. Morales,

Following up on your inquiry this morning regarding the Office of Planning and Research (OPR) filing for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project.

Our internal log shows that your Notice of Exemption packet was stamped “RECEIVED – CLERK/RECORDER” on 02/18/2026 at 4:07 PM when it was dropped off at the public counter. That is the date we will use for our Clerk/Recorder file. The OPR filing date will be based on when their Sacramento office processes the digital transmission from us, which typically occurs 1–3 business days after our intake date.

To avoid confusion, please distinguish between:
1) Date received by Sacramento County Clerk/Recorder: 02/18/2026
2) Date transmitted to OPR: tentatively scheduled for 02/20/2026 during our next batch upload
3) Date OPR reflects in its online log: often the same day as transmission, but occasionally the following business day.

We understand your concern about timing, especially given your explanation that the project is intended to slow traffic and add buffered bike lanes along 14th Avenue so that kids like your 10-year-old daughter can safely walk from your house at 4125 62nd Street to Tahoe Elementary School after that near-miss at 14th Avenue and 63rd Street in March 2023.

Once OPR confirms intake, we will forward you a copy of the OPR-stamped notice for your records.

Regards,

Stephanie Lopez
Supervising Deputy Clerk
Sacramento County Clerk/Recorder

metadata.subject: RE: OPR Filing – 14th Avenue Complete Streets Project

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Morales <daniel.morales@cityofsacramento.gov>
To: CEQA Intake <state.clearinghouse@opr.ca.gov>
Cc: amy.hsu@cityofsacramento.gov; mark.dunlevy@cityofsacramento.gov
Date: 2026-02-21 9:18 AM
Subject: Clarification on OPR Received Date – 14th Avenue NOE

Dear OPR Staff,

I’m writing to clarify how we should reference the “date received for filing at OPR” in our internal tracking for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project (City Project No. PW-14AV-24).

Our Sacramento County Clerk/Recorder stamped the Notice of Exemption as received on 02/18/2026, and their office indicates they transmitted the NOE to you on 02/20/2026 as part of a batch. For our capital program schedule, we’ve been tentatively noting 02/21/2026 as the operative date for the CEQA posting window, assuming that is when your system will show the document as received.

Because this project qualifies for a Class 1 exemption (CEQA Guidelines §15301(c)) and consists solely of minor alterations to existing streets, sidewalks, and related appurtenances along 14th Avenue with no expansion of capacity, the exact received date matters for when we can authorize construction advertising.

We’re under some pressure due to budget and personal factors: my family went down to one car in July 2022 after my father, Carlos Morales, had a stroke at Methodist Hospital of Sacramento and we took on significant medical and caregiving expenses, so I’m keenly aware of how important safer walking and biking conditions are for families relying on active transportation.

Could you please confirm which date you will list as the official OPR received date for this filing so I can align our internal forms?

Sincerely,

Daniel Christopher Morales
Senior Planner, Environmental Services
City of Sacramento Department of Public Works

metadata.subject: Clarification on OPR Received Date – 14th Avenue NOE

artifacts.content:
Title: Confirm OPR Received Date for 14th Ave NOE

Description:
Call OPR CEQA/State Clearinghouse desk to verify the official “date received for filing at OPR” for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project Notice of Exemption.

Notes for call:
- Clerk/Recorder counter stamped NOE as received: 02/18/2026 (4:07 PM)
- County staff said they transmitted NOE to OPR via electronic upload: 02/20/2026
- Amy believes OPR may log the document as received on 02/21/2026 based on past experience.
- Need to confirm which date OPR will use for the posting period and which date I should enter in the “clerkopr filing information > date received for filing at OPR” field on our internal CEQA form.
- Emphasize that the project is strictly within existing curb lines and qualifies as a Class 1 exemption; City Attorney already flagged in June 2022 that any capacity-increasing interpretation could complicate the ongoing ADA-access lawsuit filed by our neighbor Luis Herrera over his fall on a non-compliant curb ramp near 62nd Street.
- Mention that the project is time-sensitive because we are trying to get traffic-calming and buffered bike lanes in place along 14th Avenue before the next school year, after my daughter’s near-miss in the crosswalk by 63rd Street.

Location: Desk phone – 915 I Street, 3rd Floor
Reminders: 15 minutes before; follow-up task to update CEQA tracking sheet with confirmed date.

metadata.title: Confirm OPR Received Date for 14th Ave NOE

artifacts.content:
From: Amy Hsu <amy.hsu@cityofsacramento.gov>
To: Daniel Morales <daniel.morales@cityofsacramento.gov>
Date: 2026-02-23 11:06 AM
Subject: CEQA Form – OPR Date Field for 14th Ave

Daniel,

I reviewed your draft CEQA tracking sheet for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project. In the “clerkopr filing information > date received for filing at OPR” field you left a blank with a note to confirm with the State Clearinghouse.

Until we hear back from OPR, please enter 02/21/2026 as a placeholder. That’s the date we’ve typically used in the past when the County transmitted on a Friday (02/20/2026 in this case) and OPR logged the filing the next calendar day. Once they respond to your email or call, we can update the field if necessary.

For internal purposes, the important thing is that the date you record in that field matches what ultimately appears in OPR’s online log for the Notice of Exemption. The Clerk/Recorder’s received date of 02/18/2026 is already documented elsewhere on the form, so don’t duplicate it in the OPR-specific box.

I also added a short sentence in the project description about the buffered bike lanes and safety improvements near Tahoe Elementary School, referencing the near-miss involving your daughter at 14th Avenue and 63rd Street, since that clearly illustrates the purpose and beneficiaries of the project.

Please revise the form and send me the updated PDF by EOD so I can route it for signatures.

Thanks,

Amy

artifacts.content:
Title: Update CEQA Form – Enter OPR Received Date Placeholder

Description:
Block time to finalize the CEQA tracking form for the 14th Avenue Complete Streets and Safety Improvements Project.

Action items during this block:
- In the “clerkopr filing information > date received for filing at OPR” field, enter 02/21/2026 as the temporary date, per Amy’s email guidance, until OPR confirms the official received date.
- Double-check that the Clerk/Recorder received date of 02/18/2026 is only listed in the correct section and not mistakenly copied into the OPR field.
- Confirm that the project description explains the intent to slow traffic and add buffered bike lanes along 14th Avenue so kids walking from 4125 62nd Street to Tahoe Elementary (like Sofia) can do so more safely, and that it references our reliance on walking/biking after we went down to one car in July 2022 due to my dad’s stroke and resulting medical expenses.
- Verify that the exemption section clearly cites Class 1 (CEQA Guidelines §15301(c)) and notes that all work remains within existing curb lines, consistent with the City Attorney’s June 2022 guidance.

Location: Home office – 4125 62nd Street

Reminder: Pop-up 10 minutes before start so I can wrap up any ongoing calls.

metadata.title: Update CEQA Form – Enter OPR Received Date Placeholder
